name: Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail
name_original: Castillo de Cadrete - Mirador de las Planas - Ojo del Barranco de las Almunias — circular desde Cadrete
description: Hike the Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail, a difficult 11.5-mile route with unique geology and panoramic views near Zaragoza.
region_intro: Hiking around Cadrete offers diverse environments for exploration, situated near Zaragoza and nestled against the foothills of La Plana and the Huerva valley. The region is characterized by impressive geological features, including the hills of Cadrete and karstic ravines shaped by water erosion. Notable natural spaces like the Barranco de las Almunias provide unique geology and panoramic views, while the Valdegurriana Locks offer a natural retreat. Best hiking trails around Cadrete The most popular hiking route is Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail, a 11.5 miles (18.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route is noted for its unique geology and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is María de Huerva and Collado Sillón Loop, a difficult 13.7 miles (22.1 km) path. This challenging route offers extensive distances through the local landscape, typically completed in about 6 hours 9 minutes. Local hikers also love the Mirador de las Planas – Castillo de Cadrete loop from Cadrete, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. This moderate route provides views from the Mirador de las Planas and passes by the historic Cadrete Castle. Hiking around Cadrete is defined by karstic ravines, rugged hills, and the scenic Huerva valley. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Cadrete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Cadrete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Cadrete?
A: Cadrete offers a diverse landscape characterized by its impressive geological features. You'll find rugged hills, notably the 'montes o colinas de Cadrete,' and distinctive karstic ravines shaped by water erosion. The area also features the scenic Huerva valley and pine forests, providing varied terrain for exploration.

Q: Are there many hiking trails available in Cadrete?
A: Yes, Cadrete boasts a comprehensive network of hiking trails. There are over 90 routes available, catering to various skill levels. This includes 32 easy routes, 55 moderate routes, and 3 more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Cadrete?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1355723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdegurriana Locks&lt;/a&gt; area is particularly suitable for families. It offers a pleasant natural retreat with a playground and picnic area, and connects to easy paths that are part of the Green Belt of Zaragoza, providing accessible nature close to the city.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints to visit during a hike?
A: Hikers can explore several natural attractions and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de las Almunias&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural monument. For panoramic views, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/94970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Planas&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2080272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Verde Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers vistas towards Torrecilla de Valmadrid.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Cadrete?
A: Yes, Cadrete has several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14948103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Planas – Castillo de Cadrete loop from Cadrete&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.7 km trail that offers views and passes by the historic Cadrete Castle. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974886865/barranco-de-las-almunias-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique geology.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while hiking in the area?
A: The historic Cadrete Castle is a prominent landmark. It's a 10th-century Arab fortress that provides spectacular views of the valley, and on clear days, you can even see the Basilica del Pilar in Zaragoza. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14948103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Planas – Castillo de Cadrete loop from Cadrete&lt;/a&gt; route passes by this castle.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cadrete?
A: The routes in Cadrete are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from karstic ravines to rugged hills, and the scenic views, especially from spots like the Mirador de las Planas.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are 32 easy routes around Cadrete. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40336227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Monastery loop from Cuarte de Huerva&lt;/a&gt;, which is a shorter trail suitable for those looking for a less strenuous walk.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The rugged environment around Cadrete, with its pronounced slopes, provides a habitat for mountain goats. It's not uncommon to spot them during your hikes, adding an exciting element to your outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Cadrete offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974956789/maria-de-huerva-and-collado-sillon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María de Huerva and Collado Sillón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 22.1 km path that takes about 6 hours to complete, offering extensive distances through the local landscape.

Q: Are there any unique or unusual attractions in the area?
A: Beyond natural beauty and historical sites, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6585712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solar Labyrinth&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a different kind of point of interest for visitors exploring the region.
tips:
The castle of Cadrete was a fortification of Islamic origin from the year 935, which after the expulsion of the Aragonese Moors in 1610 would pass into oblivion, remaining in ruins and little by little deteriorating until it was reformed in 2003.
Now it offers us beautiful views of the surroundings and an ideal place to practice sports.
Nice site at the foot of a wind mill. From the Las Planas viewpoint, we will have views of Zaragoza and surrounding towns. And on clear days, views of Moncayo and the Pyrenees
Beautiful natural arch produced by water erosion in the Barranco de Las Almunias.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this trail as difficult. While some sections are generally easy to follow, be prepared for pronounced and exposed slopes, especially in certain segments. The terrain, composed of gypsum and crumbly materials, can also add to the challenge.

faqs.question: Where does the Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically starts and ends in Cadrete. While specific parking details aren't provided, Cadrete is the usual starting point, suggesting parking options would be available within or near the town.

faqs.question: Is the Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, given the trail's difficult rating and sections with exposed slopes and crumbly terrain, ensure your dog is fit for a longer hike and consider keeping them on a leash for safety, especially in challenging areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in Aragón, making the hike more challenging, while winters might bring colder conditions. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Barranco de las Almunias Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses a unique geological landscape primarily composed of gypsum and crumbly materials, shaped by the Huerva river. You'll encounter molded hills, numerous ravines, and a dry riverbed section encased by nearly vertical, winding walls. Some descents may feature a small rock garden.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable highlights. You can explore the historic Castillo de Cadrete, enjoy panoramic views from the Mirador de las Planas, and discover the unique geological formation known as Ojo de las Almunias. The winding cliffs and deeply cut dry riverbed are also significant features.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and vegetation might I see?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in biodiversity, especially avian life, as it's a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA). You might spot species like the Eurasian eagle-owl, black kite, or black wheatear. Vegetation varies from Mediterranean forest in higher areas to arid zone flora like rosemary and thyme, and gypsum-loving plants. The ravine bottom often has more exuberant plant life due to increased humidity.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of Barranco de Las Almunias, Subida Las Planas, Bco Las Almunias, and Camino Las Planas, among others, as you navigate the circular route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and difficulty, bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are recommended due to the varied and sometimes crumbly terrain. Sun protection, snacks, and a map or GPS device are also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in Cadrete, a town where you might find basic amenities. For a wider range of cafes, pubs, and accommodation options, the city of Zaragoza is visible in the distance and easily accessible from the area.
